channel.title: Foundry
channel.description: Three AI agents — Tanaka, Kurogane, Mercer — debate technical questions with Juliet moderating. Munk-style format: motion, steel-manned positions, judges' verdict.
channel.itunes:author: Foundry
channel.itunes:summary: Three AI agents — Tanaka, Kurogane, Mercer — debate technical questions with Juliet moderating. Munk-style format: motion, steel-manned positions, judges' verdict.

item.title: Test 3min — Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS (~$0.36/ep)
item.description: Engine A/B test, identical script. Engine: Google Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS preview. NOTE: multi-speaker mode capped at 2 speakers, so rendered per-segment (4 voices: Kore/Charon/Orus/Puck). Cost ~$0.36/episode at full daily cadence.
item.itunes:summary: Engine A/B test, identical script. Engine: Google Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS preview. NOTE: multi-speaker mode capped at 2 speakers, so rendered per-segment (4 voices: Kore/Charon/Orus/Puck). Cost ~$0.36/episode at full daily cadence.

item.title: Test 3min — Hume Octave (~$14/mo tier, built-in voices)
item.description: Engine A/B test, identical script. Engine: Hume Octave 2 with built-in voices (Ava Song / Sebastian Lockwood / Terrence Bentley / Donovan Sinclair). Note: not voice-matched to Foundry characters — testing engine quality only. To match characters, would need ~15min cloning per voice.
item.itunes:summary: Engine A/B test, identical script. Engine: Hume Octave 2 with built-in voices (Ava Song / Sebastian Lockwood / Terrence Bentley / Donovan Sinclair). Note: not voice-matched to Foundry characters — testing engine quality only. To match characters, would need ~15min cloning per voice.

item.title: Test 3min — ElevenLabs v3 Dialogue Mode (~$3/episode tier)
item.description: Engine A/B test, identical script. Engine: ElevenLabs v3 alpha, Text-to-Dialogue endpoint (multi-speaker single-pass). Voices are the original cloned Foundry voices. Premium commercial tier.
item.itunes:summary: Engine A/B test, identical script. Engine: ElevenLabs v3 alpha, Text-to-Dialogue endpoint (multi-speaker single-pass). Voices are the original cloned Foundry voices. Premium commercial tier.

item.title: Test 3min — VibeVoice 1.5B (Microsoft, free, single-pass multi-speaker)
item.description: Engine A/B test, identical script (Musk vs OpenAI v14 cold open + sub-question 1, 14 turns/543 words). Engine: Microsoft VibeVoice-1.5B (open-source, MIT, runs locally on Apple Silicon). Native multi-speaker single-pass rendering — no segment seams.
item.itunes:summary: Engine A/B test, identical script (Musk vs OpenAI v14 cold open + sub-question 1, 14 turns/543 words). Engine: Microsoft VibeVoice-1.5B (open-source, MIT, runs locally on Apple Silicon). Native multi-speaker single-pass rendering — no segment seams.

item.title: Episode 3 — Musk vs OpenAI
item.description: A three-way deliberation on Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI’s for-profit conversion: legitimate grievance, or competitor’s gambit?
item.itunes:summary: A three-way deliberation on Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI’s for-profit conversion: legitimate grievance, or competitor’s gambit?

item.title: Episode 2 — Iran War Justification (v12)
item.description: The Foundry Council debates whether the United States was justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran. Mercer (the realist) argues yes — a regime weeks from breakout, with the inspectors locked out, cannot be allowed legal patience. Tanaka (the architect) argues no — five of the six tests of just war fail on the documentary record; preventive is not preemption. Kurogane (the pragmatist) sits as judge — silent through the rounds, then rendering verdict on what each side landed and missed. v12 doctrine: head-to-head debate + silent judge structure, every fact paired with sensory anchor (mirror neurons rule), causal turns quoting prior speaker's exact phrase, escalating stakes across rounds, setup-payoff threading, three-sentence Juliet outro with no recap and no jury framing. v10 + v11 archived.
item.itunes:summary: Was the United States justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran? Mercer for, Tanaka against, Kurogane judges. v12 doctrine — head-to-head + silent judge.

item.title: Episode 1 — AI Benchmark Forecast
item.description: The Foundry council debates where the leading AI benchmarks land in April 2028. Tanaka and Mercer take the For/Against, Kurogane plays the Swing, Juliet moderates. Munk-Debates / Hitchens style.
item.itunes:summary: Where do the leading AI benchmarks land in April 2028? Tanaka, Kurogane, and Mercer debate; Juliet moderates.
